meta.topic: Claude Code hook for automatic session handoff to Qdrant
meta.project_context_summary: Windows/Qdrant/Ollama stack with slow embeddings, using a Python-based CLI hook.

summary.text: The optimal solution is to create a lightweight Python script triggered by the SessionEnd hook. This script launches an asynchronous background process to handle transcript summarization, chunking, embedding, and Qdrant storage via the existing qdrant-store-gemini.py. This async approach prevents slow Ollama embeddings (2.4s/chunk) from blocking the Claude Code CLI. The background process uses an LLM to extract decisions, blockers, code artifacts, and next steps, storing everything in the existing {meta, summary, chunks} schema.
summary.primary_recommendation: Implement a lightweight SessionEnd hook that triggers an asynchronous background script for all processing to avoid blocking the CLI.

chunks.title: Question 1: Optimal Hook Event Selection
chunks.content: Use SessionEnd as your primary hook trigger. PreCompact fires mid-session for context management and is unsuitable for final handoffs. Stop fires on interruptions (frequent, incomplete states). SessionEnd fires once when the session concludes, providing the complete, final transcript. The CLI will pass JSON to stdin containing session_id and transcript_path. This timing guarantees you're working with a complete unit of work suitable for long-term storage and retrieval.
questions_answered: Which hook events are best for capturing session state?
action_items: Configure hook in Claude Code settings

chunks.title: Question 2: Meaningful Transcript Extraction
chunks.content: Raw transcripts are verbose. For useful handoffs, distill the transcript into a structured semantic summary. Use an LLM to extract: (1) Decisions & Resolutions (final conclusions), (2) Key Code Artifacts (functions, classes, configs that were created/modified), (3) Unresolved Blockers (problems identified but not solved), (4) Agreed Next Steps (action items for future sessions), (5) Key Discoveries & Insights (important information learned), (6) Overall Session Goal (one-sentence summary). This structured summary becomes the summary.text field in Qdrant, providing a dense, searchable representation of the session's value for future retrieval.
questions_answered: What should be extracted from the transcript?
action_items: Design LLM prompt for structured extraction

chunks.title: Question 3: Database Schema Decision
chunks.content: Reuse your existing {meta, summary, chunks} schema. Creating a new specialized schema requires modifying qdrant-store-gemini.py and changing your retrieval queries. Reusing the existing schema minimizes rework and maintains consistency. Map session data as follows: meta = session metadata (session_id, creation_timestamp, project_directory, source_type='claude_session_handoff'). summary = AI-generated summary containing decisions/blockers/next-steps. chunks = turn-by-turn conversational data where each chunk is an assistant response prefixed with the preceding user query for context. This structure leverages code you've already built and tested.
action_items: Map session data to existing schema

chunks.title: Question 4: Hook Architecture Pattern
chunks.content: Use a two-stage architecture. Stage 1: on_session_end.py (the hook) is lightweight and fast. It parses JSON from stdin, extracts session_id and transcript_path, and launches process_session.py as a non-blocking background process using subprocess.Popen. It returns immediately without waiting. Stage 2: process_session.py (the processor) is the heavy lifter. It runs independently, reads the transcript file, calls an LLM to generate the structured summary, chunks the transcript by turns, formats JSON according to the {meta, summary, chunks} schema, and calls qdrant-store-gemini.py via subprocess.run. On Windows, use subprocess.Popen(command, creationflags=subprocess.DETACHED_PROCESS, close_fds=True) to ensure the processor runs in the background even if the parent process closes.

chunks.title: Question 5: Handling Slow Ollama Embeddings
chunks.content: 2.4s per chunk is critical risk. A 20-turn session would take 50 seconds synchronously. Solution: mandatory asynchronous architecture (see q4_architecture). The entire pipeline must run in a detached background process completely decoupled from the interactive CLI. The user should not wait. Secondary optimizations: (1) Model choice - benchmark nomic-embed-text against faster alternatives like mxbai-embed-large (potentially 10x faster). (2) GPU acceleration - running Ollama with NVIDIA/CUDA reduces embedding time from seconds to milliseconds per chunk. (3) Batching - modify qdrant-store-gemini.py to collect all chunks and send to Ollama in one API call if the endpoint supports batch inputs. (4) Cloud API - use Google text-embedding-004 or similar (faster, but costs money and requires internet).
questions_answered: How to handle slow Ollama embeddings?

chunks.title: Question 6: Optimal Chunking Strategy
chunks.content: Use hybrid turn-based strategy creating two document types per session: (1) Holistic Summary Document - embed the AI-generated summary as one document. Best for broad questions ('What was the API authentication session about?'). (2) Granular Turn Documents - for each assistant response, create a document. Prepend the user's preceding query to provide context. Each turn-chunk is embedded individually, allowing fine-grained retrieval ('Find the pip command for FastAPI installation'). This hybrid approach provides both high-level summaries and specific code snippets/commands. The combination optimizes for both broad discovery (via summary) and precise detail retrieval (via turns).
action_items: Embed both summary and individual turns

chunks.title: Implementation Code: Hook and Processor Scripts
chunks.content: Hook script (on_session_end.py): Parse JSON from stdin, extract session_id and transcript_path, launch processor via subprocess.Popen with DETACHED_PROCESS flag, return immediately. Processor script (process_session.py): Use argparse for session_id and transcript_path arguments. Read transcript file. Call Ollama LLM with detailed prompt extracting decisions/blockers/code/insights. Implement turn-based chunking (each turn = assistant response prefixed with user query). Format JSON as {meta, summary, chunks}. Call qdrant-store-gemini.py via subprocess.run passing JSON via stdin. Critical details: (1) Windows async: subprocess.Popen(command, creationflags=subprocess.DETACHED_PROCESS, close_fds=True). (2) Hook returns immediately after launch. (3) Comprehensive file logging to ~/.claude/logs/handoff.log (only way to debug background processes). (4) Argparse for processor arguments. (5) LLM prompt should explicitly instruct extraction of decisions, blockers, code artifacts, next steps. (6) Test end-to-end: session end should produce Qdrant document within 2 minutes.
questions_answered: How to implement the hook and processor?
action_items: Implement process_session.py with LLM and chunking

phases.title: Setup: Hook and Processor Scripts
phases.description: Create the two core Python scripts: on_session_end.py (hook trigger) and process_session.py (background processor). Implement argument parsing and asynchronous subprocess.Popen.

tasks.task: Create on_session_end.py that parses JSON stdin and launches processor asynchronously
tasks.rationale: Hook must be fast - only job is to trigger the real work in background.

tasks.task: Create process_session.py with argparse for session_id and transcript_path
tasks.rationale: Processor needs to know which session and transcript to work with.

tasks.task: Test subprocess.DETACHED_PROCESS on Windows for background execution
tasks.rationale: Windows background behavior is critical for async architecture.

phases.title: Core Logic: Summarization and Chunking
phases.description: Implement transcript reading, LLM-based summarization with detailed extraction prompt, and turn-based chunking in process_session.py.

tasks.rationale: Basic I/O prerequisite for all downstream processing.

tasks.task: Implement summarize_transcript() calling Ollama with extraction prompt
tasks.rationale: Handoff quality depends entirely on summary generation.

tasks.task: Implement chunk_transcript() for turn-based chunks with context
tasks.rationale: Turn-based chunks provide good balance of context and retrieval precision.

phases.title: Integration: Qdrant Storage
phases.description: Format data for {meta, summary, chunks} schema and integrate with qdrant-store-gemini.py. Add file logging for debugging.

tasks.task: Format session data into correct schema
Call qdrant-store-gemini.py with JSON via stdin

tasks.rationale: Background processes need file logging for debugging.

phases.title: Deployment: Configure Claude CLI
phases.description: Register hook in Claude Code settings.json for SessionEnd event. Test end-to-end workflow.

tasks.task: Locate and understand Claude Code settings.json hook schema
Add SessionEnd hook entry pointing to on_session_end.py
Test session end and verify Qdrant document appears within 2 minutes

phases.title: Optimization & Hardening
phases.description: Benchmark embedding models, implement error handling, investigate GPU acceleration.

tasks.task: Benchmark embedding models (nomic vs mxbai-embed-large vs others)
tasks.rationale: Finding faster model could reduce processing time 10x.

tasks.task: Implement error handling for LLM failures, file I/O, Qdrant connection errors
Investigate GPU acceleration for Ollama on Windows
tasks.rationale: Could improve embedding speed from seconds to milliseconds.

critical_decisions.context: Handoff quality depends entirely on summary. Local models (Llama3, Gemma via Ollama) offer privacy/no-cost. Cloud APIs (Gemini, Claude) offer higher quality/speed but cost money and egress data.
critical_decisions.recommendation: Start with llama3:8b (local, fast, good quality balance). Can upgrade to cloud API later if quality insufficient.

critical_decisions.context: 2.4s/chunk x 20 turns = 50 seconds synchronously. CLI would be unusable.
critical_decisions.recommendation: MUST use asynchronous background-process architecture. Synchronous is not viable.

critical_decisions.context: Background processes have no console output. No visibility into failures without logging.
critical_decisions.recommendation: Implement file logging to ~/.claude/logs/handoff.log. Only way to debug silent failures.

risks.mitigation: Background process could fail undetected. Implement file logging capturing all subprocess outputs and errors.
Bad summaries = useless stored data. Requires good prompt engineering and periodic review of stored summaries.
Improperly configured Windows background processes leave orphaned Python processes. Use correct subprocess.Popen flags (DETACHED_PROCESS).

success_criteria.metric: Claude Code returns control within 1 second of session end.
New Qdrant document appears within 2 minutes of session end, no manual intervention.
Semantic search for key decision/code snippet returns relevant session in top 5 results.
